purpose: Test whether generator-side curvature is an operational control signal for latent editing. All methods and negative outcomes are reported; outputs must not filter attributes or methods based on observed performance.

experiments.claim_tested: High-curvature candidate directions are unstable at matched semantic change; low-curvature selection is evaluated against random and high-curvature controls.
experiments.reporting_rule: Report all five FFHQ attributes and all three groups. Do not drop smile/pose/gender if unfavorable.

experiments.claim_tested: Curvature can be used with target response to choose causal layer subsets; canonical, all-layer, random, and wrong-layer baselines are retained as controls.
experiments.reporting_rule: Report all methods even if curvature_control underperforms. Treat underperformance as evidence about the limits of the proposed control rule.

experiments.claim_tested: Mixed-Hessian risk predicts actual composed-edit failure better than univariate curvature controls.
experiments.reporting_rule: Report all ten FFHQ attribute pairs and all predictors. Do not select a failure threshold after seeing results; use top-50 failure as fixed in the script.
